QF.id: Income, Residential Status, Five Heads of Income_6

question.text: Mr. Ramesh has a let-out property with actual rent received of Rs.4,20,000 per annum. The expected rent (after applying municipal value, fair rent, and standard rent rules) is Rs.3,80,000. Municipal taxes paid are Rs.20,000. Interest on housing loan is Rs.3,50,000. What is the income/loss from this house property under the old tax regime?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** GAV = Higher of actual rent (Rs.4,20,000) and expected rent (Rs.3,80,000) = Rs.4,20,000. NAV = GAV - Municipal taxes = Rs.4,20,000 - Rs.20,000 = Rs.4,00,000. Less 30% standard deduction = Rs.1,20,000. Net = Rs.2,80,000. Less interest on loan = Rs.3,50,000 (no cap for let-out). Loss = Rs.2,80,000 - Rs.3,50,000 = Rs.(-)70,000. **The Trap:** Some candidates apply the Rs.2 lakh interest cap meant for self-occupied property to let-out property. For let-out properties, the entire interest is deductible without any cap. Others forget the 30% standard deduction. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers house property basics; Level 2 requires full computation with the GAV determination hierarchy. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For clients with let-out properties, the interest deduction has no upper cap — unlike the Rs.2 lakh limit for self-occupied properties — making leveraged rental property a powerful tax planning tool under the old regime.
